1.1: the first figs, which are excellent.
1.16: They will put their hands over their mouths to show that astonishment and admiration render them speechless.
1.11: You will be freed from the law of the victor.
1.3: The judge He gets paid, he demands gifts from his clients. Cf. Micah ,
1.8: It is Samaria speaking to Babylon.
1.9: the city , Jerusalem or Samaria, capital of the kingdom of Israel; for it appears, by verse
1.20: . ― Geth ; city of the Philistines. ― Beth-Aphra , in Benjamin's tribe.
1.13: In this verse and the following ones, God is addressing Samaria.
1.44: ). ― the nobility of Israel ; is used here ironically to mean shame and ignominy.
1.42: ), it was only a village. — Among the thousands of Judah . The Hebrews were formerly divided into various classes, one of which consisted of a thousand men (see Exodus ,
1.12: from one sea to the other, probably the Mediterranean to the west and the Dead Sea to the east. mountain , that is to say, from the mountains of Arabia Petraea to those of Lebanon in the north.
1.14: The herd , etc.; these are the Israelites returned from captivity. Freed from the captivity of their enemies, able to support themselves without the help of others, they graze freely and without fear in the midst of their land, and in the fertile Carmel. Basan . See Numbers ,

1.24: . ― Setim , a place where the Israelites practiced idolatry with the Moabites. ― Galgala , the first encampment of the Israelites in the land of Canaan (see Joshua ,

2.1: Woe to those who plot iniquity and prepare evil on their beds. At daybreak they carry it out when it is within their power.
2.2: They covet the fields and seize them, the houses and take them; they do violence to man and his house, to the master and his inheritance.
2.3: Therefore this is what the Lord says: “I am planning an evil against this generation from which you will not be able to escape, and you will no longer walk with your heads held high, for it will be an evil time.”.
2.4: On that day a proverb will be spoken about you and a lament sung: «It is done,» they will say, “we are utterly devastated: he alienates the portion of my people. How can he take it from me? He distributes our fields to the infidels?”
2.5: Therefore, you will have no one to stretch out the measuring line in your house for a share of the inheritance in the assembly of the Lord.
2.6: "Stop prophesying," they say. "If we do not prophesy to these people, their disgrace will not depart.".
2.7: You who are called the house of Jacob, is the Lord without patience? Are these his works? Are not my words kind to him who walks uprightly?
2.8: Yesterday my people rose up as adversaries: you tear off the robe from under the robe, those who pass by without trusting, you treat as enemies.
2.9: You drive the women of my people from their beloved homes, you take away my glory from their little children forever.
2.10: Get up. Leave, for this land is not a place of rest, because of the defilement that will torment you and a cruel torment.
2.11: If there was a man running after the wind and uttering lies, saying, "I will prophesy wine and beer to you," he would be the prophet of this people.
2.12: I will gather all of you, O Jacob, I will gather the remnant of Israel, I will group them together like sheep in a fold, like a flock in the midst of its pen, they will be a noisy multitude of men.
2.13: The one who breaks through goes up before them; they break through, they go through the gate and out through it; their king goes before them, and the Lord is at their head.

3.1: I said, “Listen now, you leaders of Jacob and officials of the house of Israel. Is it not for you to know the law?”,
3.2: You who hate good and love evil, who tear the skin from their bodies and the flesh from their bones?
3.3: They devour the flesh of my people: they tear the skin off their bodies, they break their bones, they cut them to pieces like what is in the pot and like meat in a cauldron.
3.4: Then they will cry out to the Lord, but he will not answer them; he will hide his face from them at that time, according to the wickedness they have put into their actions.
3.5: This is what the Lord says about the prophets who lead my people astray, who proclaim peace while they still have something to bite on, but declare war on anyone who puts nothing in their mouths:
3.6: «"Therefore you will have night instead of visions and darkness instead of divination; the sun will set for the prophets and the day will grow dark for them.
3.7: The seers will be confounded and the diviners will blush with shame; they will all cover their beards because there will be no more answer from God.»
3.8: But I am filled with power, with the spirit of the Lord, and with judgment and courage to denounce to Jacob his transgression and to Israel his sin.
3.9: Listen to this, you leaders of the house of Jacob and officials of the house of Israel, who abhor justice and pervert what is right:,
3.10: building Zion with blood and Jerusalem with iniquity.
3.11: Its leaders judge for bribes, its priests teach for a fee, and its prophets practice divination for money; yet they rely on the Lord, saying, «Is not the Lord among us? No harm can come upon us.»
3.12: Therefore, because of you, Zion will become a plowed field, Jerusalem a heap of ruins, and the temple mount a wooded high place.

4.1: In the last days the mountain of the Lord’s house will be established as the highest of the mountains and exalted above the hills, and the nations will stream to it.
4.2: And many peoples will come and say, «Come, let us go up to the mountain of the Lord and to the house of the God of Jacob, he will teach us his ways and we will walk in his paths,» for out of Zion will go forth the law and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.
4.3: He will be the arbiter of many nations and the judge of mighty peoples far and wide. They will beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into sickles; nation will not take up sword against nation, nor will they train for war anymore.
4.4: They will remain seated each under his vine and under his fig tree, with no one to disturb them, for the mouth of the Lord of hosts has spoken.
4.5: For all peoples walk each in the name of their god, but we walk in the name of the Lord our God, always and forever.
4.6: On that day, declares the Lord, I will gather those who are lame, I will assemble those who were scattered and whom I had harmed.
4.7: And I will make of those who are lame a remnant and of those who are far off a mighty nation, and the Lord will reign over them on Mount Zion from then on and forever.
4.8: And you, tower of the flock, hill of the daughter of Zion, it will come to you, it will return to you, the former dominion, the kingdom that belongs to the daughter of Jerusalem.
4.9: Why do you cry out now? Is there no longer a king in you, and has your advisor perished, that pain has seized you like a woman in childbirth?
4.10: Be in pain and labor, daughter of Zion, like one who is in labor; for now you will go out of the city and dwell in the fields and you will go to Babylon; there you will be delivered, there the Lord will redeem you from the hand of your enemies.
4.11: And now many nations have gathered against you, saying, «Let her be desecrated, and let our eyes behold Zion.»
4.12: But they do not know the thoughts of the Lord and they do not understand his plans: knowing that he has gathered them like sheaves of wheat on the threshing floor.
4.13: Arise and throng, daughter of Zion, for I will make your horns iron and your hooves bronze, and you will crush many peoples, and I will dedicate their gains to the Lord and their riches to the Lord of all the earth.
4.14: Now, daughter of troops, gather your troops. They have laid siege to us, they strike the judge of Israel on the cheek with sticks.

5.1: And you, Bethlehem Ephrathah, though you are small among the clans of Judah, out of you will come for me one who is to be ruler over Israel, and his origins will be from of old, from days of eternity.
5.2: Therefore he will give them up until the time when she who is to give birth has given birth, and the rest of her brothers will return to the children of Israel.
5.3: He will stand firm and shepherd his sheep in the strength of the Lord, in the majesty of the name of the Lord his God, and they will dwell securely, for now he will be great, to the ends of the earth.
5.4: He will be the peace. When the Assyrian comes into our land and his foot treads upon our palaces, we will raise up against him seven shepherds and eight princes of the people.
5.5: They will rule the land of Assyria with the sword, and the land of Nimrod within its gates. He will deliver us from the Assyrian when he comes into our land and his foot treads upon our territory.
5.6: And the remnant of Jacob will be in the midst of many peoples like dew from the Lord, like raindrops on the grass, which waits for no one and does not hope in the children of men.
5.7: And the remnant of Jacob will also be among the nations, in the midst of many peoples, like a lion among the beasts of the forest, like a young lion among flocks of sheep when it passes through, tramples and tears, no one will deliver.
5.8: May your hand be raised against your adversaries and may your enemies be exterminated.
5.9: On that day, declares the Lord, I will exterminate your horses from among you and I will destroy your chariots.
5.10: I will ruin the cities of your country and demolish all your fortresses.
5.11: I will remove the spells from your hand, and there will no longer be any soothsayers in your house.
5.12: I will destroy your idols and your pillars from among you, and you will no longer worship the work of your hands.
5.13: I will uproot your sacred pillars from among you and I will destroy your cities
5.14: And in my anger and fury, I will take vengeance on the peoples who did not listen.

6.1: Listen then to what the Lord says: Arise and pour out your quarrel before the mountains, and let the hills hear your voice.
6.2: Listen, mountains, to the Lord’s quarrel with you, unchanging foundations of the earth, for the Lord has a quarrel with his people and will plead his case against Israel.
6.3: My people, what have I done to you? How have I caused you pain? Answer me.
6.4: For I brought you up from the land of Egypt, I redeemed you from the house of bondage, and I sent before you Moses, Aaron, and Miriam.
6.5: My people, remember therefore the advice that Balak, king of Moab, gave and what Balaam, son of Beor, answered him; remember Setim as far as Gilgal, so that you may know the righteous acts of the Lord.
6.6: With what shall I come before the Lord, and bow down before the God of heaven? Shall I come before him with burnt offerings, with calves a year old?
6.7: Will the Lord be pleased with thousands of rams, with myriads of rivers of oil? Shall I give my firstborn for my sin, the fruit of my womb for the sin of my soul?
6.8: He has shown you, O man, what is good, and what the Lord requires of you: to act justly, to love mercy, and to walk humbly with your God.
6.9: The voice of the Lord cries out to the city, and it is wise to heed your name: Listen to the announcement of the punishment and to him who has commanded it.
6.10: Are there still in the house of the wicked wicked treasures and a diminished, abominable ephah?
6.11: Would I be pure with unfair scales and false weights in the bag?
6.12: The rich of this city are full of violence, its inhabitants utter lies, and their tongue is nothing but deceit in their mouth.
6.13: I, in turn, will strike you with deadly blows, I will devastate you because of your sins.
6.14: You will eat, but not be satisfied, for your hunger will be within you; you will carry away, but save nothing, and what you save, I will give to the sword.
6.15: You will sow, but you will not reap. You will press the olives, but you will not anoint yourself with oil; the grape juice, but you will not drink the wine.
6.16: You observe the ordinances of Amri and all the practices of the house of Ahab, and you walk according to their advice, so that I may deliver you to destruction and the inhabitants of the land to jeering, and that you may bear the reproach of my people.

7.1: Alas, I am like after the harvest of summer fruits, like after the gleaning of the grape harvest: not a bunch to eat, not one of the first figs that my soul desires.
7.2: The pious man has disappeared from the earth, and there is no longer a righteous man among mankind. They all lie in wait to shed blood; each hunts down his brother and lays a net for him.
7.3: Evil has hands to do good; the prince demands, the judge sets his price, and the great man displays his greed; that is how they plot together.
7.4: The best of them is like a thorn, the most upright, worse than a bramble hedge. The day announced by your watchmen, the day of your punishment has come; now they will be in confusion.
7.5: Do not believe a friend, do not trust a close confidant before the one who rests at your breast; guard the words of your mouth.
7.6: because the son calls his father a madman, the daughter rebels against her mother, the daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law, each person has as enemies the people of his own household.
7.7: And I will look to the Lord, I will hope in the God of my salvation; my God will hear me.
7.8: Do not rejoice over me, O my enemy, for though I have fallen, I shall rise again; though I sit in darkness, the Lord is my light.
7.9: I will bear the Lord’s wrath because I have sinned against him, until he takes up my cause and establishes my right; he will bring me out into the light, I will behold his righteousness.
7.10: Let my enemy see it and let shame cover her, she who said to me, "Where is the Lord your God?" My eyes will behold her, now she will be trampled underfoot like the mud of the streets.
7.11: The day will come when your walls will be rebuilt, on that day the decree will be revoked.
7.12: On that day, people will come to you from Assyria and from the cities of Egypt, from Egypt to the river, from sea to sea and from mountain to mountain.
7.13: The country will become a desert because of its inhabitants: this is the result of their actions.
7.14: Shepherd your people with your shepherd's staff, the flock of your inheritance that dwells alone in the forest, in the midst of Carmel, that it may find its pasture again in Bashan and Gilead as in days of old.
7.15: Just as on the day you came out of the land of Egypt, I will show him wonders.
7.16: The nations will see it and be ashamed of all their power; they will put their hands over their mouths, their ears will be deafened.
7.17: They will lick the dust like a snake, like the reptiles of the earth they will come out in terror from their hiding places, they will come trembling to the Lord our God and they will fear you.
7.18: Who is a God like you, who pardon iniquity and forgive transgression from the remnant of your inheritance? He does not always retain his anger, but delights to show mercy.
7.19: He will again have pity on us, he will again trample our iniquities underfoot. You will cast all their sins into the depths of the sea,
7.20: You will show Jacob your faithfulness, and Abraham the mercy you swore to our fathers from of old.
